stat_of_week: 0.38 WHIP - Jacob Misiorowski posted a 0.38 WHIP over 5.1 IP for the Riverside Roughnecks, the most dominant single-game pitching line of the week and one of the most untouchable performances of the season
thriller_note: Hiroshima survived by the thinnest of margins despite North Correa's Emerson Hancock and Chris Sale both posting 1.29 ERAs and 0.86 WHIPs in the same week, a remarkable rotation performance that still wasn't enough — and with Hiroshima's luck delta in negative territory, this narrow win may be masking real vulnerability heading into a date with the red-hot Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs.
key_storyline: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs finally broke through in dominant fashion, winning 8-2 over a Sugar Land Skeeters team that entered the week as the consensus top squad in the league. The Wonderdogs posted the highest team OBP of the season at .419, a new league record, while Mickey Moniak, Pete Alonso, and Ozzie Albies all delivered multi-HR performances in a lineup that looked every bit like a contender. The victory is validation for a roster that had been bleeding expected wins all season with a -0.69 luck delta — but the timing of that breakout coming against the Skeeters carries maximum standings impact.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers carry the most inflated actual-vs-expected win delta in the entire league at +1.58, and this week's 6-2 loss to the Roughnecks was propped up by a .336 OBP and just one quality start from a rotation that remains among the league's weakest on paper.
unlucky_reason: Despite finally winning big this week, the Wonderdogs entered Week 6 with the worst luck delta in the league at -0.69, meaning their strong category outputs over the season have repeatedly failed to convert into the wins their performance deserved.
records_broken: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs set a new season record for highest team OBP at .419, surpassing the previous mark of .388 held by Sugar Land Skeeters from Week 1.
power_rankings: 1. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The 8-2 demolition of the Skeeters — with a new season-record .419 OBP, 12 HR, and 38 runs — is the kind of statement week that reshapes a season; the luck pendulum has finally swung and this lineup looks dangerous | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | An 8-2 blowout loss is jarring for the preseason favorite, but Chase Burns' 16 Ks, Matt Olson's 3 HR, and a 7 SB week remind you the talent floor here is still elite; one bad week doesn't erase five strong ones | down

3. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kazuma Okamoto's 4 HR and 11 RBI, Ben Rice's 3 HR, and 12 team homers — the most explosive offensive week in the league this week — fueled a 6-4 win over Canberra; this team has been sleeping and just woke up | up

4. BaseballPro77 | Ranger Suarez was borderline untouchable at 0.50 WHIP with 13 Ks over 12 IP, Jordan Walker drove in 11, and the 7-3 win was convincing; a Week 7 showdown with General Hospital is the biggest game of the season so far | same

5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Luzardo (1.35 ERA, 18 Ks), Clay Holmes (0.71 ERA), and 10 team quality starts are the most dominant pitching week any team has had all season; the 6-3 win was built on rotation excellence | up

6. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge's 3 HR and .556 OBP headlined a 7-2 win that showed what this lineup can do; Peter Lambert replaces Liberatore but the rotation still needs more before anyone trusts it in a playoff spot | same

7. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton's 5 HR week was a reminder of his ceiling and Zack Wheeler's 1.50 ERA kept things competitive, but the 7-3 loss to BaseballPro77 revealed real pitching depth concerns beyond Wheeler | down

8. General Hospital | The 6-3 loss stings despite 35 runs scored and a Vladdy-led offense generating real thunder; Max Fried was solid, but the team ERA of 3.76 and WHIP of 1.42 signal the rotation outside Fried isn't holding up its end — Jared Jones is a critical add | down

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Misiorowski's 0.38 WHIP, Langeliers' .542 OBP, and a disciplined 6-2 win over the Benchwarmers make this one of the quieter success stories of the week; the rotation is slowly becoming an asset | up

10. North Correa | Hancock and Sale each posted 1.29 ERAs in the same week and still lost 5-4 — that kind of rotation luck inversion is maddening; Bryson Stott and Luis Severino are smart adds but the offense needs to hit to support this staff | same

11. Hiroshima Radiation | A 5-4 squeaker over North Correa is a win, but 73 team strikeouts and only 3 HR and 14 RBI from the lineup is a troubling offensive imbalance; the pitching is real but this team cannot sustain without run support | same

12. Canberra Wombats | Pete Crow-Armstrong was excellent and Dylan Cease logged 12.2 innings, but 23 runs scored and a .316 OBP tell the story of an offense that simply isn't generating enough; Jake Bennett is a sensible rotation add but this team needs bats | down

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Bryan Reynolds' .469 OBP and Parker Messick's 15 Ks were bright spots in a 7-2 loss, but 22 runs scored and a 4.01 ERA say this roster is stuck in neutral; Spencer Steer is a useful add but not a difference-maker | same

14. Benchwarmers | The +1.58 luck delta is the loudest alarm bell in the league — Yesavage's 0.96 ERA was a genuine positive but one quality start, a 4.07 ERA, and an offense that scored 25 runs all week cannot sustain a winning record | same
